Hero.trustBadge: Coming soon · within the regulatory sandbox of the General Authority for Real Estate
Hero.headline: Own a Fraction of Saudi Arabia's Real Estate Future
Hero.subheadline: A Saudi platform for owning fractional shares in selected real estate, with official ownership registration and Sharia-compliant terms, under regulatory supervision.

How.headline: Five steps to your first share
How.step1Body: Leave your email and number to be among the first registered.
How.step2Title: Be first to know at launch
How.step2Body: We notify you first when the platform opens, before opportunities go public.
How.step3Body: A quick national ID verification, in line with regulator requirements.
How.step4Body: Browse the available opportunities and pick what fits your goals, starting from an accessible amount.
How.step5Body: Periodic rental distributions, with full transparency on fees and figures.

Why.headline: Built to reassure before it returns
Why.benefit1Body: Your share is documented by an official title deed, registered with the relevant authorities. Not just a record on a platform.
Why.benefit2Body: Product structure and distributions are subject to ongoing Sharia review.
Why.benefit3Body: Start with a small share and spread your investment across multiple properties instead of concentrating in one asset.
Why.benefit4Title: Full transparency on fees and returns
Why.benefit4Body: Every fee, every distribution, every expected return is shown in clear figures before any decision.
Why.benefit5Body: We handle leasing, maintenance, and collection. You don't deal with tenants or contractors.
Why.benefit6Body: Clear exit mechanisms when available, allowing liquidity when needed under defined terms.

Privacy.headline: Privacy Policy
Privacy.sub: We're committed to protecting your personal data in line with the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL).
Privacy.s1Body: Osoul.Tech is a Saudi platform for fractional real estate ownership operating within the regulatory sandbox of the General Authority for Real Estate. We comply with the Personal Data Protection Law (PDPL) of the Kingdom and treat your data as a serious responsibility from day one.
Privacy.s2Body1: We collect the data you voluntarily submit through the interest form:
Privacy.s2List1: Full name, mobile number, email, and city.
Privacy.s2List2: Additional data depending on your registration type: expected investment amount, preferred property type, property details, company name, and position.
Privacy.s2List3: Technical data collected automatically: IP address, browser type, pages visited, and visit timestamps.
Privacy.s3List1: To contact you about the platform launch and updates on relevant opportunities.
Privacy.s3List2: To send introductory content tailored to your registration type (investor, owner, partner).
Privacy.s3List3: To improve user experience and develop the platform.
Privacy.s3List4: To comply with regulations and instructions issued by supervisory authorities.
Privacy.s4Body1: We do not sell your data. We share it only with:
Privacy.s4List1: Service providers that enable the platform to operate (hosting, databases, email services).
Privacy.s4List2: Regulatory or judicial authorities upon a formal lawful request.
Privacy.s4Body2: In every case, we work with partners who meet equivalent data-protection standards.
Privacy.s5Body: We encrypt sensitive data in transit and at rest, and limit internal access to authorized personnel only under strict access controls. We review security procedures periodically.
Privacy.s6Body: Under PDPL, at any time you have the right to:
Privacy.s6List1: Access the data we hold about you.
Privacy.s6List4: Withdraw your consent to data processing.
Privacy.s6Foot: To exercise these rights, contact us at info@osoul.tech.
Privacy.s7Body: We use a limited set of cookies for analytics and to improve site performance. You can configure your browser to refuse cookies, although some features may not work fully in that case.
Privacy.s8Body: We may update this policy from time to time. We will notify you of material changes via email or a clear on-site notice well in advance of taking effect.
Privacy.s9Body: For privacy-related enquiries or to exercise your rights, contact us at info@osoul.tech.

Terms.headline: Terms of Service
Terms.sub: Please read these terms carefully before using the Osoul.Tech site.
Terms.s1Body: By using the Osoul.Tech site, you acknowledge that you have read and agreed to these terms. If you do not agree, please do not use the site.
Terms.s2Body: In its current phase, the Osoul.Tech site offers a single, defined service: registering users' interest in the platform launch. No investment account, mobile app, or trading platform is currently available. The full service (fractional property ownership and share management) will be made available at official launch within an approved regulatory framework.
Terms.s3List1: Provide accurate and current information in the registration form.
Terms.s3List2: Do not impersonate others or use false data.
Terms.s3List3: Respect the intellectual property rights of Osoul.Tech and other parties.
Terms.s3List4: Do not attempt to compromise site security or the integrity of its data.
Terms.s4Body: All content on the site, including the logo, brand, text, design, and code, is owned by Osoul.Tech or its licensors. Re-use, copying, or distribution of any part is not permitted without prior written consent.
Terms.s5Body1: The site is provided 'as is' without warranties of any kind, express or implied. The content published is not an investment recommendation and should not be treated as a substitute for independent financial advice.
Terms.s5Body2: Osoul.Tech is not liable for any decision taken based on information on this site before the investor signs a formal contract with the platform at launch.
Terms.s6Body: These terms and any disputes arising from them are governed by the laws of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, with jurisdiction vested in the competent courts of the Kingdom.
Terms.s7Body: We reserve the right to amend these terms at any time. Changes take effect from the date of publication, and we will notify you of material changes appropriately.
Terms.s8Body: For any legal enquiry, contact us at info@osoul.tech.

Risks.headline: Risk Disclosure
Risks.sub: Please read this disclosure carefully before making any investment decision when the platform launches.
Risks.s1Body: Real estate investing, like any investment, involves risk. The returns shown on the platform are estimates based on market data and existing contracts, not guarantees. Past performance of any property or opportunity is not a reliable indicator of future performance.
Risks.s2Body: Property values may fluctuate up or down depending on macroeconomic conditions, shifts in supply and demand, or regulatory changes. This volatility may affect both the market value of your share and the exit return.
Risks.s3Body: Real estate is inherently illiquid. You may not be able to sell your share immediately when you wish to. Expected holding periods are typically between 1 and 5 years, and may extend under certain conditions.
Risks.s4List1: Delayed receipt of rents or default by some tenants.
Risks.s4List2: Higher maintenance costs or unexpected expenses.
Risks.s4List3: Vacancy periods between leases that may reduce the actual return.
Risks.s5Body: Real estate, tax, or supervisory regulations may change in ways that affect investment opportunities or operating mechanics. Legal disputes related to a property or its parties may also arise.
Risks.s6Body: The platform relies on digital infrastructure. While we follow leading cybersecurity practices, technical risks (downtime, cyberattacks, human error) remain a real possibility that should be considered.
Risks.s7List1: Read each opportunity's prospectus carefully before participating.
Risks.s7List2: Consult an independent financial advisor when needed.
Risks.s7List3: Do not invest amounts you cannot afford to lose.
Risks.s7List4: Diversify your portfolio rather than concentrating capital in a single opportunity.

About.heroHeadline: Empowering Saudis to own real estate, share by share
About.heroSub: Osoul.Tech is a Saudi platform that lets individuals own fractional shares in selected properties, with officially registered title deeds and Sharia-compliant terms, under the regulatory supervision of the General Authority for Real Estate.
About.nameLayer1Body: Real estate as tangible assets, owned and managed and producing real income, not virtual numbers.
About.nameLayer2Body: Belonging, land, and place. A Saudi name in native Arabic, for a platform built in the Kingdom to serve its people.
About.nameLayer3Body: Integrity, clarity, and Sharia adherence. The fundamentals that don't change, no matter how the tools do.
About.mvvHeadline: What we aim for, and how we work
About.missionBody: Make real estate investment accessible to everyone, with affordable amounts and officially registered title deeds.
About.visionBody: To be the first digital gateway for fractional real estate ownership in the Kingdom.
About.trait1Body: Operating under regulatory supervision and with full transparency. We speak the language of institutions, not the language of hype.
About.trait2Body: Built in the Kingdom, speaking Arabic natively. We are not a translated copy of a Western platform.
About.trait3Body: A fast, clear, mobile-first experience. Technology in service of trust, not as a showcase.
About.trait4Body: Polished investment expertise, available to the smallest investor as much as to the largest.
About.regaHeadline: Within the regulatory sandbox of the General Authority for Real Estate
About.regaBody: Osoul.Tech operates inside the regulatory sandbox of the General Authority for Real Estate. The sandbox lets us test the model under direct regulator oversight and within clearly defined boundaries before any public launch, ensuring investors a measured and supervised environment from day one.
About.regaTag: Supervised by the General Authority for Real Estate

How2.heroHeadline: From registration to returns, step by step
How2.heroSub: We map the full path before you take the first step. Every stage is defined, documented, and supervised.
How2.step1Body: Before launch, leave your details to join the waiting list. We notify you first when the platform opens, and we never share your data with third parties.
How2.step2Body: A quick sign-up via Nafath. No paperwork, no in-person visits. Your account is ready in minutes, with identity verified by official channels.
How2.step3Body: KYC and AML checks in line with regulator requirements, protecting both you and the wider investment system.
How2.step4Body: Browse carefully selected opportunities, review the prospectus, location, valuation, and expected return. Then pick a share that fits your budget.
How2.step5Body: Periodic rental distributions deposited into your account, plus the possibility of capital appreciation when the property is sold at the end of the holding period.
How2.sukukBody: Your share in every property is registered as an official title deed in your name with the real estate registry, not just as a record on the platform. That registration is the difference between real ownership of a tangible asset and digital promises with no backing.
How2.sukukPoint1: Deed registered in the investor's name
How2.returnsRentalBody: A portion of the property's rent is distributed periodically (monthly or quarterly depending on the property) to owners in proportion to their shares. Full distribution details appear in your account.
How2.returnsCapitalBody: The possibility that the property's value rises during the holding period, realized when the property is sold at the end of the term. Not guaranteed, but part of the investment logic of real estate.
How2.returnsHoldingBody: Typically 1 to 5 years, set per opportunity and disclosed up front in the investment prospectus.
How2.exitHeadline: Flexible exit at the right time
How2.exitBody: Every opportunity has a holding period announced in advance, ending with the sale of the property and distribution of capital proceeds to owners. We also provide an in-platform early-exit option when a willing investor buyer is available, under defined terms and transparent pricing.
How2.exitPoint1Body: Each opportunity comes with a known term (typically 1 to 5 years). No open-ended commitments.
How2.exitPoint2Body: Sell your share early when a buyer is available, under a transparent pricing mechanism.
How2.exitPoint3Body: At the end of the holding period the property is sold and proceeds are distributed.

Shariah.heroSub: We operate under continuous oversight from an independent Sharia committee, and no product launches without its approval of the structure, mechanisms, and contracts.
Shariah.whyBody: Islamic finance is not about renaming things. It is a model based on partnership in a real asset, not interest-bearing debt. Your share in an Osoul.Tech property represents actual ownership of a tangible asset that produces income from real rent, under a clear partnership contract. No riba, no gharar, no hidden synthetic product.
Shariah.whyPoint1: Real ownership of a tangible asset
Shariah.committeeNote: The actual committee members will be announced before launch. The profiles below indicate the scope of expertise.
Shariah.member1Bio: Expert in Islamic finance and banking, researcher in the application of fiqh to modern financial instruments.
Shariah.member2Bio: Specialist in fiqh of transactions and financing, with contributions to the structuring of compliant investment products.
Shariah.member3Bio: Professor of comparative fiqh and Islamic sukuk, has served on Sharia boards of major financial institutions.
Shariah.structure1Body: The joint-ownership structure between owners in each property, in proportion to each share. Every owner is an actual partner in the asset, not a creditor.
Shariah.structure2Body: The property is leased to tenants under a Sharia-compliant lease contract, with rent distributed to owners in proportion to their shares after operating expenses.
Shariah.structure3Body: Usufruct rights over the property are clearly defined in the partnership contracts, preserving each owner's rights without breaching Sharia limits.
Shariah.auditBody: Sharia compliance is not a one-off decision at launch. Our committee reviews every new opportunity before it is offered, periodically audits distribution mechanisms and operating contracts, and issues an annual report on the platform's compliance.

Contact.heroHeadline: We're here to answer your questions
Contact.heroSub: Before the official launch, we take investor and partner enquiries through the channels listed below. We reply within one business day.
Contact.emailNote: We reply within 24 hours on business days.
Contact.whatsappNote: Available Sunday to Thursday, 9am to 5pm Riyadh time.
Contact.faqBody: Browse the FAQ; you may find your answer there directly.
Contact.registerBody: If you'd like to join the first cohort of users at launch, use the registration form.

Faq.heroSub: Direct, documented answers to the most frequent questions. Search, or browse by category.
Faq.noResults: No questions match your search. Try a different word or browse the full categories below.
Faq.fallbackBody: Reach out to us directly, and we'll respond within one business day.
Faq.g1A: Osoul.Tech is a Saudi platform that lets individuals own fractional shares in selected properties, backed by official title deeds. The platform operates within the regulatory sandbox of the General Authority for Real Estate, and its products are compliant with Islamic Sharia.
Faq.g2Q: How is fractional ownership different from traditional real estate investing?
Faq.g2A: Traditional investing requires significant capital to buy and manage a whole property yourself. Fractional ownership lets you own a small share of a fully managed property, so you receive rental income without dealing with tenants or maintenance, and you can spread your capital across several properties.
Faq.g3Q: Who can invest on the platform?
Faq.g3A: At launch the platform will be available to individuals resident in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia who meet identity and AML requirements. Any later expansion to other investor segments will be announced via official channels.
Faq.g4Q: When will the platform officially launch?
Faq.g4A: We are currently operating within the regulatory sandbox of the General Authority for Real Estate. We will announce the official launch date directly to those on the interest list first, before any public announcement.
Faq.g5Q: Is the platform safe and trustworthy?
Faq.g5A: We operate under direct regulatory supervision from the General Authority for Real Estate, and your ownership is recorded as an official title deed with the relevant authorities, not only inside the platform. We also follow rigorous data-protection and information-security standards.
Faq.o1Q: Do I get an official title deed?
Faq.o1A: Yes. Every share you hold is registered as an official title deed in your name in the real estate registry, in proportion to your share in the asset. The deed is verifiable with the relevant authorities, not just a platform entry.
Faq.o2Q: How is my ownership of a share evidenced?
Faq.o2A: Your ownership is evidenced in three layers: the official title deed registered with the relevant authorities, the partnership contract signed between you and the other owners, and the platform account record reflecting both.
Faq.o3Q: Can I own shares in more than one property?
Faq.o3A: Yes. Diversification is the platform's core idea. You can spread your capital across several properties in different cities and segments (residential, commercial, retail) to reduce concentration risk and diversify income sources.
Faq.o4Q: What is the minimum to invest?
Faq.o4A: The platform is designed to be accessible at a low minimum. The final minimum per opportunity will be announced at launch and will appear clearly in the prospectus of each opportunity.
Faq.r1A: The distribution cadence varies by property type and the underlying lease contracts, often monthly or quarterly. The cadence for each opportunity is disclosed in its prospectus before you invest.
Faq.r2Q: How is the return rate calculated?
Faq.r2A: Net rental return is calculated from gross rent minus operating expenses and management fees. Each opportunity prospectus shows the figures in detail: gross return, expenses, management fees, then expected net.
Faq.r3A: Returns are not guaranteed. The figures shown are estimates based on existing leases and market data, but they remain subject to occupancy and price variability. That is part of the nature of real estate investing.
Faq.r4Q: What is the difference between rental and capital returns?
Faq.r4A: Rental return is periodic income (monthly or quarterly) from leasing the property. Capital return is realized at the end of the holding period if the property's value has risen at sale. Rental is recurring and predictable, capital is one-time and not guaranteed.
Faq.e1Q: When can I sell my share?
Faq.e1A: At the end of the announced holding period for each opportunity (typically 1 to 5 years) the property is sold and proceeds are distributed. We also provide an in-platform early-exit mechanism when a buyer is available, within defined windows.
Faq.e2Q: How is a share priced when sold?
Faq.e2A: For an early in-platform exit, the price is based on the latest official valuation and a transparent pricing mechanism. At end-of-term, the actual sale price of the property is distributed to owners in proportion to their shares.
Faq.e3A: Any fees will be announced in full in the fee document before launch. Our principle is that all platform fees are disclosed up front and in clear figures, with no later surprises.
Faq.s1Q: Who oversees the products from a Sharia perspective?
Faq.s1A: Our products are overseen by an independent Sharia committee of scholars specialized in fiqh of transactions and Islamic finance. Member names and bios will be announced before the official launch.
Faq.s2A: Yes, and no product is offered on the platform without the committee's approval of its structure. Contracts and distribution mechanisms are subject to ongoing Sharia review, and an annual compliance report is issued.
Faq.s3Q: What is the approved Sharia structure?
Faq.s3A: We rely on three core concepts: Musharakah (joint ownership), Ijarah (leasing the property), and Intifa' (usufruct rights). The structure for each opportunity is detailed in its specific prospectus.
Faq.a1Q: How is my personal data protected?
Faq.a1A: We comply with data-protection regulations in the Kingdom and never share your data with any third party for marketing purposes. Internal data access is restricted to authorized personnel only, under strict access controls.
Faq.a2A: We use industry-standard encryption for all sensitive data in transit and at rest, and work with licensed payment partners and banks. Your card details are never stored on our servers.
Faq.a3Q: What if I lose access to my account?
Faq.a3A: A secure recovery flow will be available via Nafath identity verification, plus human support from our customer service team. Your title-deed ownership is unaffected by losing platform access, since it is registered in your name outside the platform.
